details.title: Outdoor Products Ultimate Waterproof Lightweight Dry Bag, 3 pack, Unisex, Green, Blue, 10.6 L
details.description:
• Outdoor Recreation Group - Set of 3 Ultimate Outdoor Light-weight Dry Bags:
  • Colors: Blue and Green
  • Waterproof Lightweight Dry Bag
  • Outdoor products pack ultimate dry sacks are super compact and ultra lightweight
  • Polyurethane-coated for ultimate water and weather resistance
  • Roll top allows maximum compression
  • Roll top closure allows for maximum compression
  • Double stitched and watertight taped sealed seams help keep gear dry
  • Soft and flexible material for easy packing
  • Includes one of each sized bags:
  • 2 liter bag - 12.7" x 7.5"
  • 4 liter bag - 15.1" x 9.2"
  • 10 liter bag - 21.5" x 10.5"
  • Total Capacity: 16 L
  • Use to hold your clothing, electronics, maps, cooking utensils, knives and more
  • Easy to place in your vehicle for a trip outdoors
  • Each is sized differently to accommodate your various needs and organization
  • Use to take a small vacation with the family
  • Not for storing food or water
  • Not intended for full submersion or flotation
  • Additional accessories shown in images not included with purchase

all_reviews.reviewText: Love these bags and have a couple of sets.  I use them for kayaking and backpacking to keep my gear dry and organized.  I have never had any issues with wet gear using them.  The different sizes and colors make it easy to keep items of all sizes secure.

all_reviews.reviewText: Great bags for the money. I had to toss these early after purchase.  I would love to buy again if I get a chance. Light weight and great for spring rain.

all_reviews.reviewText:
I'm packing to redeploy home and had some more expensive stuff sacks issued to me. I needed more stuff sacks but didn't want to pay huge amounts. These are nearly 1/3 cheaper than others I've seen online, plus I get three in a package . Three T-shirts and my twin comforter fit into the largest sack! I am thrilled that I can now fit things in my bags and won't have to ship as much home, not to mention I can reuse these and they weigh practically nothing and take up no space when empty.
It is definitely work to cramm so much into each one, but it is worth the effort. It took nearly two weeks to receive them at my APO address.

all_reviews.reviewText:
I purchased the dry sacks with the understanding that they are water resistant but not waterproof. Contents that had to stay dry went into zip top bags. I used them on a three day kayak camping trip on the Great Lakes and they performed better than I expected. In rough seas, water entered the hatches but the contents remained protected.

The dry sacks worked well, have great small sizes and cannot be beat at the price. It would be nice if there were more colors to help code the contents.

all_reviews.reviewText:
The bags come in 3 sizes and a bonus phone case was included.  Each bag is a different color, so if you buy multiple sets you could color code your use. These are smaller sized bags and good for keeping items like a camp stove dry.  

I bought these to use in our camp cooking tub and they are perfect for that purpose.  The smallest one will hold the backpacking stove.  The middle sized one will hold extra fuel canisters.  The large sized one will hold several freeze dried and other shelf stable meals.  

My youngest daughter would take these backpacking with her.  My backpacking days are long over and I use mine in a tub with other cooking items for camping.

Other uses would be to pack clothing, camping equipment and other items you want to keep dry while traveling.

I like these bags and have bought sets for both my daughters.

all_reviews.reviewText:
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.] While these are not designed to be able to withstand a complete submersion or be used and depended on to float or be used as a flotation device, they are actually very waterproof for such a light weight fabric. Think lightweight rain jacket to visualize the fabric. 
They carry a full 70D waterproof rating, which has a  hydrostatic head (HH) rating of 2000 mm means that a fabric can withstand a column of water that is 2 meters (2000 mm) tall before leaking. This rating is often used to measure the waterproofness of fabrics, such as tents and jackets.
A  a jacket with an HH rating of 2000 mm is suitable for moderate rain and everyday use.

all_reviews.reviewText: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I love waterproof dry sacks.  I first started using them for camping and kayaking, and now I use them for all travel.  It is awful when you get caught in the rain while traveling, and get your things soaked.  These bags allow you to keep your phone, wallet and other dry. I have even used these bags in cases where the bag was almost fully submerged (not the use case) and they still stayed dry.  These particular dry bags are excellent.  The fabric is highly waterproof, the roll-top is smooth and tight and the three sizes are perfect.  The largest will fit some clothes you want to keep dry or even a pair of sneakers, I put food for long hikes in the medium bag, and the small bag can easily hold my phone, keys and wallet.  Definitely worth investing in to make outdoor adventures and even general travel more comfortable. And, the price on this set is great for the quality.
all_reviews.itemName: Outdoor Products Ultimate Waterproof Lightweight Dry Bag, 3 pack, Unisex, Green, Blue, 10.6 L

I really like the packaging they come in and how efficiently they are packed.  They could be used to store things in the car or really anywhere. all_reviews.reviewText: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I own several dry bags, but these new ones are made from a lightweight nylon material, similar to parachute fabric, which makes them a great complement to my heavier rubber dry bags. The largest bag is perfect for storing a beach towel and a change of clothes when heading to the pool or shoreline. The medium-sized bag is ideal for protecting electronics like my iPad and speaker from water and sand. The smallest bag fits conveniently in the glove box, ready for anything unexpected. While these bags aren't designed for submersion like my others, they offer excellent protection on a boat, during vacations, in suitcases, at the beach, or on hikes, so the weight and size are a good trade off for not being submersible. They are easy to use—simply roll, curl, and click. Overall, these dry bags are a great addition to my collection, and I would recommend them.

all_reviews.reviewText:
2 stars for their intended purpose; 5 stars for their actual value as an organization system. Went back to buy more for mine and my husband's backpack gear.

So I bought these little fellers to go along with me as I backpacked. Thought it would be good to have a set of bags to keep delicate valuables safe. My first adventure was a vacation to Costa Rica. I used the large orange one to hold all my camera equipment. Could anything be much more valuable? Doubt it, so waterproof housing was a must! As it turns out, we didn't run into a wet situation in Costa Rica, so there was no test there. Still, the orange helped me keep track of where my go-to items were.
Next adventure was a cruise. I wanted to capture my boys as they frolicked in the Caribbean waters off the coast of Jamaica. Long story short, I removed the camera to take pictures--fortunately--as all the other items in the bag bobbed along the surface of the water attached to my arm. When I got through, there was about an inch of water inside the bag and everything left in it was wet. Keep in mind, it merely bobbed for about a half-hour...never submerged. Waterproof? Not even!!!!
However, these bags DO serve a GREAT purpose! I have since used them to separate the components inside my hiking backpack and they are unbeatable! They make MUCH better ditty bags than the ditty bags WM sells for that specific purpose because you can roll them over compression-style to accommodate the necessary size of whatever you're storing, then clip them into place. I'll be returning my designated ditty bags to WM for these little guys, and should I run into a rain, I hope they'll at least keep my valuables dry from a DAMP environment.
all_reviews.reviewTitle: Waterproof? HA! But don't discount their value..

all_reviews.reviewText:
I absolutely love these little babies - I have purchased multiple 3-packs of these over the years and use them for week-long canoeing and backpacking trips. When new, they are completely waterproof...as long as they are packed appropriately and the roll-down top is "married up" together well. If you have a lot of extra space in them and the top is loose they will certainly leak.

The three sizes are great for camping - the smallest blue ones will hold ample amounts of food such as the oatmeal, granola, coffee, tea, powdered milk and sweetener for two people for a week. The orange ones are great for lunches, snacks and dinners for two people for a similar amount of time, although we dehydrate much of our food so the size and weight is significantly decreased. The largest green ones are used for clothing - you can compress your clothing into those and they end up feeling like a solid rock of clothes (not a comfy pillow). I have easily fit four each of socks, underwear and t-shirts along with a long-sleeved shirt and lightweight pants. Sometimes there's even enough room for a fleece. Since I've purchased six or seven of these over the years, everyone gets one or two of them to pack their clothes in and we always find a way to stuff a little more.

Over time, they will eventually develop small holes due to rough usage (tossing them on the ground at the campsite, for instance). These holes are virtually insignificant unless you are going to have them submerged for an extended period (> 10 minutes) of time. Even then the impact would be small and result in only a damp area on one piece. This has never happened to me.

Lightweight, dry, easy to identify (we write on them with permanent markers to identify contents) and highly effective. That's a strong recommendation.
all_reviews.itemName: Outdoor Products Ultimate Waterproof Lightweight Dry Bag, 3 pack, Unisex, Green, Blue, 10.6 L

all_reviews.reviewText: I like the fact that these sacks are color coded, which makes it easier for finding things. The sacks are of good quality material that is air tight. The stitching is of good quality as well. I like the fact that you can keep a little air inside in case of tipping a canoe, you stuff will float. My only concern is how long the clips will last. all_reviews.reviewText: We had one of these sacks in one of our kayaks which was dumped over and submerged in water for several minutes. I had put a roll of toilet paper in a heavy duty freezer Ziploc bag inside of one of these dry sacks and the toilet paper did stay dry... thanks to the Ziploc bag because there was a couple inches of water inside the bottom of the sack. On the other hand, in another kayak we had another dry sack with a towel inside of it and with this sack just getting a little water splashed onto it, the towel inside stayed completely dry. So, just to be safe, I would always put all of your contents in a Ziploc bag first, making sure it's a strong bag that pushes today to close, not the kind that slides over to close.

all_reviews.reviewText:
So far so good.  

First to address some of the complaints.  These are fairly small.  You won't fit a beach towel in them.  But if you bother to look at the sizes in the description, you'd know that.  

They are also very light weight nylon with a PVC coating on the inside.  These are not the heavy vinyl dipped bulletproof bags you find at the marine store, or maybe here.  

As such, I'd not use them for a 2 week canoe tour.  But they are great for backpacking because of their lightness.  

Also, the waterproofedness seems to be unreliable based on some of the reviews.  I don't know aboutyou, but I wouldn't put anything valuable in ANY waterproof case unless I had tested it.  Easy.  Put a paper towel in the bag and submerge it.  

Mine are fine.  Nice and waterproof.  But I could imagine if something sharp was inside the bag and it wore on the relatively thin urethane coating, the waterproofing would fail.  

So I guess its all about expectations.  If you are looking for a bag to put your cell phone in during a 3 week white water kayaking trip, this is not your bag.  If you want a dry bag to go in your backpack to protect some toilet paper, your phone and some emergency clothes, then after testing, this is great.

negative_reviews.reviewText: We had one of these sacks in one of our kayaks which was dumped over and submerged in water for several minutes. I had put a roll of toilet paper in a heavy duty freezer Ziploc bag inside of one of these dry sacks and the toilet paper did stay dry... thanks to the Ziploc bag because there was a couple inches of water inside the bottom of the sack. On the other hand, in another kayak we had another dry sack with a towel inside of it and with this sack just getting a little water splashed onto it, the towel inside stayed completely dry. So, just to be safe, I would always put all of your contents in a Ziploc bag first, making sure it's a strong bag that pushes today to close, not the kind that slides over to close.

negative_reviews.reviewText:
2 stars for their intended purpose; 5 stars for their actual value as an organization system. Went back to buy more for mine and my husband's backpack gear.

So I bought these little fellers to go along with me as I backpacked. Thought it would be good to have a set of bags to keep delicate valuables safe. My first adventure was a vacation to Costa Rica. I used the large orange one to hold all my camera equipment. Could anything be much more valuable? Doubt it, so waterproof housing was a must! As it turns out, we didn't run into a wet situation in Costa Rica, so there was no test there. Still, the orange helped me keep track of where my go-to items were.
Next adventure was a cruise. I wanted to capture my boys as they frolicked in the Caribbean waters off the coast of Jamaica. Long story short, I removed the camera to take pictures--fortunately--as all the other items in the bag bobbed along the surface of the water attached to my arm. When I got through, there was about an inch of water inside the bag and everything left in it was wet. Keep in mind, it merely bobbed for about a half-hour...never submerged. Waterproof? Not even!!!!
However, these bags DO serve a GREAT purpose! I have since used them to separate the components inside my hiking backpack and they are unbeatable! They make MUCH better ditty bags than the ditty bags WM sells for that specific purpose because you can roll them over compression-style to accommodate the necessary size of whatever you're storing, then clip them into place. I'll be returning my designated ditty bags to WM for these little guys, and should I run into a rain, I hope they'll at least keep my valuables dry from a DAMP environment.
negative_reviews.reviewTitle: Waterproof? HA! But don't discount their value..

positive_reviews.reviewText:
I absolutely love these little babies - I have purchased multiple 3-packs of these over the years and use them for week-long canoeing and backpacking trips. When new, they are completely waterproof...as long as they are packed appropriately and the roll-down top is "married up" together well. If you have a lot of extra space in them and the top is loose they will certainly leak.

The three sizes are great for camping - the smallest blue ones will hold ample amounts of food such as the oatmeal, granola, coffee, tea, powdered milk and sweetener for two people for a week. The orange ones are great for lunches, snacks and dinners for two people for a similar amount of time, although we dehydrate much of our food so the size and weight is significantly decreased. The largest green ones are used for clothing - you can compress your clothing into those and they end up feeling like a solid rock of clothes (not a comfy pillow). I have easily fit four each of socks, underwear and t-shirts along with a long-sleeved shirt and lightweight pants. Sometimes there's even enough room for a fleece. Since I've purchased six or seven of these over the years, everyone gets one or two of them to pack their clothes in and we always find a way to stuff a little more.

Over time, they will eventually develop small holes due to rough usage (tossing them on the ground at the campsite, for instance). These holes are virtually insignificant unless you are going to have them submerged for an extended period (> 10 minutes) of time. Even then the impact would be small and result in only a damp area on one piece. This has never happened to me.

Lightweight, dry, easy to identify (we write on them with permanent markers to identify contents) and highly effective. That's a strong recommendation.

positive_reviews.reviewText:
So far so good.  

First to address some of the complaints.  These are fairly small.  You won't fit a beach towel in them.  But if you bother to look at the sizes in the description, you'd know that.  

They are also very light weight nylon with a PVC coating on the inside.  These are not the heavy vinyl dipped bulletproof bags you find at the marine store, or maybe here.  

As such, I'd not use them for a 2 week canoe tour.  But they are great for backpacking because of their lightness.  

Also, the waterproofedness seems to be unreliable based on some of the reviews.  I don't know aboutyou, but I wouldn't put anything valuable in ANY waterproof case unless I had tested it.  Easy.  Put a paper towel in the bag and submerge it.  

Mine are fine.  Nice and waterproof.  But I could imagine if something sharp was inside the bag and it wore on the relatively thin urethane coating, the waterproofing would fail.  

So I guess its all about expectations.  If you are looking for a bag to put your cell phone in during a 3 week white water kayaking trip, this is not your bag.  If you want a dry bag to go in your backpack to protect some toilet paper, your phone and some emergency clothes, then after testing, this is great.

They are a bit on the small size, so cannot stuff too many items (or larger items) into them, but they still work well and do the job of keeping your belongings dry.
positive_reviews.itemName: Outdoor Products Ultimate Waterproof Lightweight Dry Bag, 3 pack, Unisex, Green, Blue, 10.6 L

positive_reviews.reviewText: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I own several dry bags, but these new ones are made from a lightweight nylon material, similar to parachute fabric, which makes them a great complement to my heavier rubber dry bags. The largest bag is perfect for storing a beach towel and a change of clothes when heading to the pool or shoreline. The medium-sized bag is ideal for protecting electronics like my iPad and speaker from water and sand. The smallest bag fits conveniently in the glove box, ready for anything unexpected. While these bags aren't designed for submersion like my others, they offer excellent protection on a boat, during vacations, in suitcases, at the beach, or on hikes, so the weight and size are a good trade off for not being submersible. They are easy to use—simply roll, curl, and click. Overall, these dry bags are a great addition to my collection, and I would recommend them.

positive_reviews.reviewText:
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I am really suprised at these light weight bag's I stuffed the large blue bag with news paper sealed the bag as to instructions and ran water over all the seams and pushed hard on it under water tho see if I could see any air bubbles coming out and found nothing. positive_reviews.reviewText:
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I was surprised at the material. I don't know what I expected, something thicker,, like vinyl. However, I gave it five stars because as you can see in one of the pictures, I ran water over one of the bags and let it sit for about 5 minutes. Dried off the outside and then ran a paper towel along the inside and there was no moisture, so the material definitely works. 

I like that it came with three sizes so you're not rolling it up 15 times with a small item but have room for larger items as well. The closure is sturdy and clips securely. I don't know about prolonged exposure to moisture, but I figured after 5 minutes if there was no moisture on the inside, we are good to go!

positive_reviews.reviewText: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I like these dry sacks from Outdoor Products. I got a combo pack of 3 dry sacks: Small - 2 liter, Medium - 4 liter and a large one at 10.5 liter. The bags are extremely lightweight. Which worried me a little since I was thinking that they might be very cheaply made. I was wrong. They seem to be made fairly well. Stitched straight and tight with the inside seam taped or laminated neatly and precise. I tested the smallest bag by putting a stack of papers folded up inside and the rolled the top 3 times ( the minimum the say to do). Then I laid it out on the lawn and sprayed it for a few minutes to over simulate a torrential down pour. Passed with flying colors. Even though the company said that the sacks are not made for submersion, I decides to test it anyway. I stuffed the largest sack with a bunch of clothes and submerged it in my bathtub for 5 minutes. Another complete success. I definitely recommend these dry sacks for all normal use.
positive_reviews.itemName: Outdoor Products Ultimate Waterproof Lightweight Dry Bag, 3 pack, Unisex, Green, Blue, 10.6 L

positive_reviews.reviewText:
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I recently picked up the Ultimate Dry Sack 3-Pack, and it’s quickly become one of my favorite travel accessories. This set comes with three sizes—Small (12.7in x 7.5in), Medium (15.1in x 9.2in), and Large (21.5in x 10.5in)—so you have just the right amount of flexibility for different needs.

The 70D waterproof fabric is a game-changer. I recently took these sacks on a camping trip where we encountered unexpected rain. I was able to keep my phone, camera, and even my favorite makeup essentials completely dry, which was a huge relief. The Small size is perfect for my toiletries, while the Medium one is great for snacks and small gadgets. The Large size came in handy for storing extra clothes and my beach towel when I went to the pool.

What I really appreciate is how durable these sacks are. They’ve held up well through various adventures, from beach outings to hikes. Plus, they’re surprisingly lightweight, so they don’t add any bulk to my luggage.

Overall, the Ultimate Dry Sack 3-Pack has been a fantastic investment. Whether you’re heading to the beach, the mountains, or just want to keep your stuff organized at home, these sacks have got you covered. They offer great protection for your gear and keep everything neat and dry.

positive_reviews.reviewText: I'm an active outdoorsman. I use these to store all my gear in my backpack(i have three sets). If you get caught in a downpour without a waterproofed backpack these will make up the difference. They are not advertised as waterproof! But I capsized our canoe with one of these in a day pack and it kept what was inside dry. There was a little bit(a small area semi moist) inside the bag but it was submerged for about 15 minutes. I took them all home dried and cleaned them and treated them with waterproofing spray(also bought at walmart). It has made up the difference and I have not had a problem. I will still keep electronics in a seperate plastic bag inside these as they are not advertised waterproof. I live in The pacific northwest and it gets wet you do all you can to stay dry. Spray everything, backpack ,storage etc with silcone spray, where one fails the next will pick up the slack.

I use mine to keep food and cloth dry kayaking,  I like that they are thin and light.  if I really need to insure its dryness I will use my heavy duty bags, but for low risk and low consequence items this is perfect,  will stay fully dry in rain, sprinklers, splash situations.  my only complaint is since changing the packaging the bag sizes aren't accurate.  box says you get 2 liter 4 liter 8 liter.  I would say you get 2 liter 2.5 liter and 8 liter.  the medium bag is not double the size of the small.

positive_reviews.reviewText: I didn't try submursing these dry sacks. I did however take them out on a speed boat while it was raining. We got spray from the water, and hard hitting rain drops and my book inside was still dry as anything. These went faithfully to the beach everyday for a week, and everyday got put back in a bag full of wet things (snorkel gear, towels) for the walk back to the hotel (5 minutes), and my stuff was never wet. Good sizing.

positive_reviews.reviewText: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] Brought these to Yosemite, on the Merced River, floating in inner tubes, to hold my phone in the small one, a small air compressor and battery in the large, and tshirts and glasses in the medium. Secured with 3+ folds as directed, but there was still significant leak through in all bags. Not saturation, but everything was still somewhat wet, though none of the bags were ever fully submerged in the water. I still think they are sturdier than a ziploc, and better than nothing.

positive_reviews.reviewText: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] These bags seem to do the job.  The smallest is quite small.  The material is not what I expected.  It's a rain jacket type material.  Very lightweight and flexible.  They stand up to a hose and being dunked in a sink.  I have not used them on a camping trip yet.  I would suggest some other sustained tests to validate prolonged submersion.  For the cost and canoeing type activity these would probably fit the bill. For Kayaking or some activity where constant submersion would be probable you may want to be careful.

positive_reviews.reviewText: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] These come in 3 different sizes -  2-liter, 4-liter, and 8-liter sacks .  They are pretty durable as well. I like that they are all different colors so you can distinguish which one is which and the material is soft and flexible making it easy for you to roll them up and close them.  I would say that they are water resistant rather than waterproof.  I wouldn't recommend submerging them in a lot of water, but they'd be perfect for keeping out some raindrops.

positive_reviews.reviewText: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] This set is made of lightweight ripstop fabric that has a thin waterproof coating inside, like windbreaker fabric. They will protect from rain but not submersion. They are for small items only, i.e. snack food, portable speaker, books. The largest bag is tubular, about half the size of a sleeping bag sack. The smaller ones are flat so hold very small items. I thought they could protect some things in my leaky garage, but they are too small for anything in there. To seal the tops, you must roll it over itself a few times then hook the clasps, which makes a circle-top that takes up much bag space. I think a plastic zip seal would be better, but on the upside, the ring-top can be hung on a backpack or jacket loop or hook while hiking, as these bags have no hangar loops. Small light-duty only.
